content.question: What proportion of Hungary's exceptional between-school variance is attributable to tracking design itself, versus interaction with free school choice, residential segregation, and governance decentralization? Can these be empirically separated?
content.reduction_path: Decomposition analysis using hierarchical variance models on Hungarian PISA/school-level data, stratifying by choice regime (catchment vs. choice schools), ethnic composition, and track type, to estimate independent and interactive contributions to between-school variance
